description: Buffer / layer-level render settings. These control how each effect layer's pixel buffer is sized, transformed, and rotated before the effect renders into it. Serialized with the B_ prefix; CopySettingsMap at src-core/render/Effect.cpp:690 strips the prefix before passing to PixelBuffer::SetLayerSettings (src-core/render/PixelBuffer.cpp:2077). Note: the Buffer panel also surfaces CHOICE_LayerMethod, SLIDER_EffectLayerMix, CHECKBOX_LayerMorph, and CHECKBOX_Canvas, but those are documented in shared/Timing.json — this file covers only buffer-shape / transform / rotation settings.

properties.label: Render Style
properties.description: Determines how the model's node layout is projected into a 2D pixel buffer. Default uses the model's configured buffer style; Per Preview renders through a 3D camera view; As Pixel collapses the model to a single-pixel buffer. The actual dropdown is populated at runtime from the model's supported styles, so additional values may appear.

properties.label: Buffer Stagger
properties.description: Percent offset applied to effect start time across nodes to create a cascading appearance. Negative values stagger backward in time; 0 disables staggering.

properties.label: Camera
properties.description: Camera to project the model through when BufferStyle is 'Per Preview'. Populated at runtime from the set of defined 3D cameras in the preview setup.

properties.label: Transformation
properties.description: Applies a fixed geometric transform to the buffer coordinate system before the effect renders. Useful for mounting an effect designed for one orientation onto a differently-oriented model.

properties.label: Blur
properties.description: Box blur applied to the rendered buffer. 1 is no blur; higher values progressively blur each pixel with its neighbors. Supports value curves for animated blur.

properties.label: Persistent
properties.description: When enabled, the buffer is not cleared between frames — each frame's render composites onto the previous one. Used by effects like 'Life' and 'Shimmer' that need running state.

properties.label: Z Rotation
properties.description: Static Z-axis rotation as a 0-100 percentage of a full turn (RZ_ROTATION_MIN/MAX in src-core/render/ValueCurveConsts.h). 0 = no rotation, 100 = full 360 degrees. For animated multi-turn rotation, use Rotations.

properties.label: Z Rotations
properties.description: Number of full Z-axis rotations over the effect duration. Stored as an integer; the render engine divides by 10 for 0.1-rotation precision (max value 200 = 20 full rotations).

properties.label: Z Pivot Point X
properties.description: X coordinate of the Z-rotation pivot as a percentage of buffer width. 50 is the horizontal center.

properties.label: Z Pivot Point Y
properties.description: Y coordinate of the Z-rotation pivot as a percentage of buffer height. 50 is the vertical center.

properties.label: Zoom
properties.description: Buffer zoom factor. Stored as an integer; the render engine divides by 10 so 10 = 1.0x (no zoom), 20 = 2.0x, max 30 = 3.0x.

properties.label: Zoom Quality
properties.description: Number of interpolation samples used when zooming. Higher values give smoother zoom at the cost of render time.

properties.label: X Rotation
properties.description: Fixed rotation around the X (horizontal) axis in degrees. Applied in 3D with Y/Z rotations according to RotationOrder.

properties.label: X Pivot Point
properties.description: Pivot coordinate for X-axis rotation as a percentage of buffer height. 50 is the center.

properties.label: Y Rotation
properties.description: Fixed rotation around the Y (vertical) axis in degrees. Applied in 3D with X/Z rotations according to RotationOrder.

properties.label: Y Pivot Point
properties.description: Pivot coordinate for Y-axis rotation as a percentage of buffer width. 50 is the center.

properties.label: Application Order
properties.description: Order in which X, Y, and Z rotations compose when any 3D rotation is non-zero. Different orders produce different final orientations because rotation is non-commutative.

properties.label: Sub-Buffer
properties.description: Restricts the effect to a rectangular subset of the model buffer. Serialized as a compact 'x1 y1 x2 y2' string (with optional rotation and per-corner kerf) by the SubBufferPanel widget. Empty means the full buffer.

notes: RotationOrder is only semantically meaningful when XRotation or YRotation is non-zero; the UI doesn't gate it, so it's just a no-op in the 2D case.

Authoritative slider ranges live in src-core/render/ValueCurveConsts.h (BLUR_*, RZ_*). The wxSmith file does not always mirror those values — e.g. the Z Rotation slider has no explicit <min>/<max>, relying on the wx default of 100 which happens to match RZ_ROTATION_MAX.
XRotation and YRotation are 0-360 (RZ_XROTATION_MAX / RZ_YROTATION_MAX), but Z Rotation is 0-100 (RZ_ROTATION_MAX). They're inconsistent by design — Z is percentage-of-full-turn, X/Y are degrees.
SLIDER_Rotations (plural) is stored with divisor 10 (RZ_ROTATIONS_DIVISOR): raw 0-200 represents 0.0-20.0 full rotations over the effect duration.
SLIDER_Zoom is stored with divisor 10 (RZ_ZOOM_DIVISOR): raw 0-30 represents 0.0-3.0x zoom.

Layer-level settings (LayerMethod, EffectLayerMix, LayerMorph, Canvas, fade in/out, transitions, palette, brightness, etc.) are also read by PixelBuffer::SetLayerSettings but are contributed by the Timing and Color panels — see shared/Timing.json and shared/Color.json.
SubBuffer is managed by a dedicated SubBufferPanel sub-control inside BufferPanel; its serialized format is opaque to this schema (a custom string parsed by PixelBuffer).

All SLIDER_* entries that list 'valueCurve: true' are persisted as a companion B_VALUECURVE_<id> serialized key when the user activates the curve; the engine's GetValueCurveInt/Double fallback reads the slider value when no curve is present.
